title: taraxacum circense
intro: Taraxacum circense, also known as the crevice dandelion or lesser dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus Taraxacum. It is a perennial plant with a rosette of leaves and a yellow flower head, often found in dry, rocky areas and meadows. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial for treating digestive issues, fever, and infections. Taraxacum circense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as an ingredient in herbal remedies and supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
